label: Chanokh — Part 1 — Chapters 1–36 — Book of the Watchers
summary_plain: The Book of the Watchers tells what happened before the flood. Two hundred angels called the Watchers saw the daughters of men and desired them. Their leader Shemikhazah led them down to Mount Hermon, where they swore an oath. They took wives and fathered the Nephilim — giants who devoured the food of humanity and then turned on the people themselves. Azazel taught men to make swords, knives, shields, and breastplates; other Watchers taught enchantments, astrology, and forbidden knowledge. The earth cried out. The four archangels — Mikha'el, Uriel, Rafa'el, and Gavri'el — looked down, saw the blood and lawlessness, and brought the report to YHWH. The Most High commanded: bind Azazel and cast him into darkness; destroy the Nephilim; send the flood through Noakh. Chanokh was sent to tell the Watchers their petition was denied. Then Chanokh was taken up and saw the throne of the Great Glory — the Creator surrounded by fire and light. He journeyed to the ends of the earth and saw the places of judgment for the fallen.
summary_scholarly: Chapters 1 through 36 comprise the Book of the Watchers, the earliest section of the Chanokh corpus. The ACR reads from the Dead Sea Scrolls Aramaic manuscripts — 4QEn-a (4Q201), 4QEn-b (4Q202), and 4QEn-c (4Q204) — and the Orit Ge'ez of the African Israelite community, which preserves the complete text. The Watchers narrative expands what Bereshit 6:1-4 states briefly: 'the sons of The Creator came in to the daughters of man.' Here the full account is given: 200 Watchers descend on Mount Hermon under Shemikhazah's leadership, swear a mutual oath, take human wives, and father the Nephilim. Azazel's teaching of metallurgy and weaponry is named as the origin of violence and corruption on earth. The four archangels — Mikha'el, Uriel, Rafa'el, Gavri'el — intercede before the Most High. The judgment is pronounced: Azazel is bound, the Nephilim are destroyed, and the flood cleanses the earth. Chanokh's throne vision in chapter 14 is one of the earliest descriptions of the divine throne in the preserved Hebrew and Aramaic literature. This is primary scripture preserved continuously by the African Israelite community and attested at Qumran.

key_terms.definition: Angels — 'the children of heaven' — who descended to earth, took human wives, and fathered the Nephilim. 200 in number. Led by Shemikhazah. They swore their oath on Mount Hermon (ch 6).
The leader of the Watchers. He feared the others would not go through with the descent and proposed the mutual oath on Mount Hermon (6:3). He is bound and imprisoned until the day of judgment.

A Watcher who taught men to make swords, knives, shields, and breastplates, and made known to them the metals of the earth (8:1). YHWH commands Rafa'el to bind him and cast him into darkness (10:4). Connected to the Yom Kippur scapegoat in Vayikra 16.
The giant offspring of the Watchers and human women. They 'consumed all the acquisitions of men' and when that was not enough, turned on humanity itself (7:2-4). The flood was sent to destroy them.
The mountain where the 200 Watchers descended and swore their mutual oath (6:6). The name Hermon is connected to the Hebrew word for oath (cherem).
One of the four archangels who saw the bloodshed on earth and brought the report to the Most High (9:1). Anglicized as Michael.
The archangel commanded to bind Azazel (10:4). His name means 'healer of The Creator.' Anglicized as Raphael.
One of the four archangels. Commanded to destroy the offspring of the Watchers — the Nephilim — by turning them against each other (10:9). Anglicized as Gabriel.
The archangel sent to warn Noakh of the coming flood (10:1). His name means 'light of The Creator.' He guides Chanokh through the cosmic journeys.

What Chanokh sees in his throne vision (ch 14): a great house of crystal with a floor of fire, and within it a lofty throne. The Great Glory sat upon it — 'his raiment shone more brightly than the sun and was whiter than any snow' (14:20).

fill_blank.prompt: And the angels, the children of heaven, saw and lusted after them, and said to one another: Come, let us choose ______ for ourselves from among the children of men.
fill_blank.source_quote: And the angels, the children of heaven, saw and lusted after them, and said to one another: Come, let us choose wives for ourselves from among the children of men and beget us children.

fill_blank.prompt: And Shemikhazah, who was their ______, said unto them: I fear that you will not indeed agree to do this deed.
fill_blank.source_quote: And Shemikhazah, who was their leader, said unto them: I fear that you will not indeed agree to do this deed, and I alone shall have to pay the penalty of a great sin.

fill_blank.prompt: And Azazel taught men to make swords, and knives, and shields, and breastplates, and made known to them the ______ of the earth.
fill_blank.source_quote: And Azazel taught men to make swords, and knives, and shields, and breastplates, and made known to them the metals of the earth and the art of working them.

fill_blank.prompt: And then Mikha'el, Uriel, Rafa'el, and Gavri'el looked down from ______ and saw much blood being shed upon the earth.
fill_blank.source_quote: And then Mikha'el, Uriel, Rafa'el, and Gavri'el looked down from heaven and saw much blood being shed upon the earth, and all lawlessness being wrought upon the earth.

fill_blank.prompt: And the women became pregnant, and they bare great ______, whose height was three thousand ells.
fill_blank.source_quote: And the women became pregnant, and they bare great giants, whose height was three thousand ells.

fill_blank.prompt: Behold, he comes with ten thousands of his holy ones to execute ______ upon all.
fill_blank.source_quote: Behold, he comes with ten thousands of his holy ones to execute judgment upon all, and to destroy all the ungodly.

fill_blank.prompt: And Chanokh went and said: Azazel, you shalt have no peace — a severe ______ has gone forth against you to put you in bonds.
fill_blank.source_quote: And Chanokh went and said: Azazel, you shalt have no peace — a severe sentence has gone forth against you to put you in bonds.

fill_blank.prompt: Go to Noakh and tell him in my name: Hide yourself and reveal to him the end that is approaching, that the whole earth will be ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: Go to Noakh and tell him in my name: Hide yourself and reveal to him the end that is approaching, that the whole earth will be destroyed, and a deluge is about to come upon the whole earth, and will destroy all that is on it.

fill_blank.prompt: Chanokh, a righteous man whose eyes were opened by The Creator, who had the vision of the Holy One and of ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: Chanokh, a righteous man whose eyes were opened by The Creator, who had the vision of the Holy One and of heaven, which he showed me.

fill_blank.prompt: And they were in all two hundred, who descended in the days of Yered on the summit of Mount ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: And they were in all two hundred, who descended in the days of Yered on the summit of Mount Hermon.

multiple_choice.question: Who led the 200 Watchers in their descent to earth?
multiple_choice.source_quote: And Shemikhazah, who was their leader, said unto them: I fear that you will not indeed agree to do this deed.

multiple_choice.question: What did Azazel teach humanity?
options: How to make swords, knives, shields, breastplates, and work metals
multiple_choice.source_quote: And Azazel taught men to make swords, and knives, and shields, and breastplates, and made known to them the metals of the earth and the art of working them.

multiple_choice.question: Where did the Watchers descend and swear their oath?
multiple_choice.source_quote: And they were in all two hundred, who descended in the days of Yered on the summit of Mount Hermon.

multiple_choice.question: Who were the Nephilim?
options: The giant offspring of the Watchers and human women
multiple_choice.source_quote: And the women became pregnant, and they bare great giants, whose height was three thousand ells.

multiple_choice.question: Which four archangels saw the bloodshed and reported to the Most High?
options: The four Watchers of the corners
multiple_choice.source_quote: And then Mikha'el, Uriel, Rafa'el, and Gavri'el looked down from heaven and saw much blood being shed upon the earth.

multiple_choice.question: What was YHWH's command regarding Azazel?
options: Bind him and cast him into darkness
Make him a guardian of the earth
multiple_choice.source_quote: And again YHWH said to Rafa'el: Bind Azazel hand and foot, and cast him into the darkness.

multiple_choice.question: Who was sent to warn Noakh of the coming flood?
multiple_choice.source_quote: Then said the Most High, the Holy and Great One spoke, and sent Uriel to the son of Lamekh.

multiple_choice.question: What happened when Chanokh brought the Watchers' petition to YHWH?
options: It was denied — a severe sentence was pronounced
multiple_choice.source_quote: And Chanokh went and said: Azazel, you shalt have no peace — a severe sentence has gone forth against you to put you in bonds.

multiple_choice.question: How many Watchers descended?
multiple_choice.source_quote: And they were in all two hundred, who descended in the days of Yered on the summit of Mount Hermon.

multiple_choice.question: What does Chanokh say YHWH will do when he comes?
options: Execute judgment upon all and destroy all the ungodly
Gather the nations for a feast
multiple_choice.source_quote: Behold, he comes with ten thousands of his holy ones to execute judgment upon all, and to destroy all the ungodly.

faq.question: Who were the Watchers?
faq.answer: Angels — 'the children of heaven' — who looked down from heaven, saw the daughters of men, and desired them (6:1-2). Two hundred of them descended on Mount Hermon in the days of Yered and swore a mutual oath under Shemikhazah's leadership. They took human wives, fathered the Nephilim, and taught forbidden knowledge. This is the full account behind the brief statement in Bereshit 6:1-4.

faq.question: Why is Azazel important?
faq.answer: Azazel taught humanity the arts of war — swords, knives, shields, breastplates, metallurgy (8:1). The text attributes the rise of violence on earth directly to his teaching. YHWH commands Rafa'el to bind Azazel and cast him into darkness until the day of judgment (10:4-6). The Yom Kippur ritual in Vayikra 16 sends a goat to 'Azazel' in the wilderness — the Chanokh text reveals who Azazel is.

faq.question: What is the throne vision in chapter 14?
faq.answer: Chanokh sees a great house made of crystal with walls and floor of fire. Within it is a lofty throne. The Great Glory sits upon it — 'his raiment shone more brightly than the sun and was whiter than any snow.' No angel can enter or look upon his face. Chanokh falls on his face, trembling, and is called forward. This is one of the earliest preserved descriptions of the divine throne in Aramaic or Hebrew literature.

faq.question: How does this connect to Bereshit?
faq.answer: Bereshit 6:1-4 says: 'the sons of The Creator came in to the daughters of man and they bore children to them. These were the mighty men who were of old, the men of renown.' The Chanokh text tells the full story: who the Watchers were, how many descended, where they took their oath, what they taught, what the Nephilim did, and why the flood came. The flood narrative in Bereshit 6-9 cannot be fully understood without this text.

faq.question: What are the DSS witnesses for the Book of the Watchers?
faq.answer: 4QEn-a (4Q201), 4QEn-b (4Q202), and 4QEn-c (4Q204) — all written in Aramaic. These are physical manuscripts from Qumran Cave 4 dating to the second and first centuries BCE. The Orit Ge'ez of the African Israelite community preserves the complete text — the Qumran fragments attest the same text in the land itself.

faq.question: Why was Chanokh's petition for the Watchers denied?
faq.answer: Chanokh wrote a memorial petition on behalf of the Watchers, asking for forgiveness and length of days (ch 13). He was told: their petition was denied. Azazel would have no peace. A severe sentence was pronounced. The Watchers who were supposed to intercede for humanity had instead corrupted it — they who should have petitioned for men were now reduced to having a man petition for them, and even that was rejected.

faq.question: Why was Chanokh excluded from both the Rabbinical and Roman Christian canons?
faq.answer: The Rabbinical canon excluded Chanokh at the Council of Yavneh (c. 90 CE) — the institution founded by Yochanan ben Zakkai in negotiation with Roman General Vespasian. The Roman Christian canon excluded Chanokh at Nicaea (325 CE). Both institutions — otherwise competitors — excluded the same text. Chanokh is among the most well-represented texts at Qumran (4QEn-a through 4QEn-g). It is preserved complete only in the Orit Ge'ez of the African Israelite community — the Beta Israel of Ethiopia. The texts both institutions excluded are the ones most completely preserved by the African transmission chain.

faq.question: What is the blood ascending in 1 Chanokh 9?
faq.answer: 1 Chanokh 9:1-3 (Ge'ez Orit, confirmed in Qumran Aramaic): 'Michael, Uriel, Raphael, and Gabriel looked down from heaven and saw much blood being shed upon the earth and all lawlessness being wrought upon the earth. And they said one to another: Let the cries of their voice ascend to the gates of heaven.' The text describes an active cosmic legal accounting. Blood shed against the covenant people accumulates as a forensic charge — heard, recorded, and eventually addressed. 1 Chanokh 47:1-2 confirms: the blood of the righteous ascends before YHWH of Spirits. Nothing is lost. Nothing is forgotten.

faq.question: What does the Watcher tradition reveal about the origin of imperial power?
faq.answer: Every domain of knowledge enabling autonomous human power outside YHWH's governance is listed in the Watcher account. Azazel transmits weapons manufacturing. Semyaza transmits occult manipulation. Baraqel, Kokabel, and Ezeqeel transmit astrology and divination. The text's specific statement is that the apparatus of human imperial power — military, intelligence, social manipulation — was transmitted to humanity through cosmic rebellion against YHWH's order. The Book of Giants (4Q203, 4Q530-532) names Gilgamesh as a Nephilim — directly connecting Mesopotamian dynastic mythology to the Watcher framework.
